draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer conversational, human tone in CRM interactions; messaging should feel like talking to a helpful friend rather than a software manual
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer polished, professional tone; casual language might feel unprofessional or undermine trust in a business tool

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality
evidence.implication: Users prefer conversational, human tone in CRM interactions; messaging should feel like talking to a helpful friend rather than a software manual

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual card layouts over dense tables when managing client relationships
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values scannable, visual-first client overview; likely prefers cards, tiles, or compact summaries across CRM surfaces
facade.rejectImplies: User gravitates toward tables, spreadsheet-style layouts, or detailed row-based data; values density and traditional data presentation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, immediate feedback (no loading states) over smooth transitions when tracking client work and payments
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values responsiveness and zero-latency UX; likely wants crisp, direct interactions without choreography; may prefer minimal animation
facade.rejectImplies: User values perceived smoothness and reassurance; likely wants transitions and motion to guide the eye; may find instant changes jarring or cold

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from soft, approachable lighting and color that feels personal rather than corporate.
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: The freelancer audience wants interfaces that feel human and inviting, possibly with warmer color palettes, softer shadows, and less harsh contrast.
facade.rejectImplies: The product should lean toward cooler, crisper, more neutral or professional aesthetics — clean grays, high contrast, minimal warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to gentle, low-contrast interfaces that feel approachable rather than clinical
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers interfaces with subtle depth, muted tones, and reduced glare—likely values calm aesthetics and eye comfort in tools they use daily
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through high contrast, crisp edges, and bright backgrounds—or sees shadow/depth as unnecessary visual noise in a work tool

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client navigation (sidebar) combined with scannable project cards rather than a dense list or tab-based layout
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content density (sidebar vs tabs + cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible navigation and visual breathing room; prefers spatial organization over sequential tabs; likely wants to jump between clients quickly
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer compact layouts, top/bottom tab navigation, or list-based dense formats that maximize viewport for content; hesitant about sidebar real estate cost

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
Whether they favor sidebar/drawer navigation vs. top tabs vs. floating action buttons—reveals how they want to access client lists, invoices, and projects in their workflow
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
Whether they prefer a calm, focused atmosphere vs. one that feels energetic and motivating—reveals if they want a tool that reduces friction or one that celebrates wins
Whether they want dense dashboards showing all metrics at once vs. progressive disclosure with drill-down details—reveals how they prefer to consume client and financial data
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals if they value speed and directness or polish and reassurance in the interface

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor sidebar/drawer navigation vs. top tabs vs. floating action buttons—reveals how they want to access client lists, invoices, and projects in their workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer a calm, focused atmosphere vs. one that feels energetic and motivating—reveals if they want a tool that reduces friction or one that celebrates wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want dense dashboards showing all metrics at once vs. progressive disclosure with drill-down details—reveals how they prefer to consume client and financial data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals if they value speed and directness or polish and reassurance in the interface
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working solo seek personality and humor in tools to offset isolation and make daily CRM tasks feel less mechanical
facade.label: Playful and quirky
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace humor, unexpected delight, personality quirks in UI copy and interactions; freelancers want tools that feel like collaborators with personality, not sterile software
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward professional, straightforward voice; freelancers prioritize efficiency and clarity over entertainment; personality should be minimal and understated
